description: The noexec mount option can be used to prevent binaries from being executed out of /boot. Add the noexec option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot.
rationale:
The <tt>/boot</tt> partition contains the kernel and the bootloader. No
binaries should be executed from this partition after the booting process
finishes.
ocil_clause: the "/boot" file system does not have the "noexec" option set
ocil:
Verify the noexec option is configured for the /boot mount point, run the following command: 
$ sudo mount | grep '\s/boot\s'
. . . /boot . . . noexec . . .
title: Add noexec Option to /boot
